violations.text: Chowder was observed held at 118 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the hoods have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The condensate from the freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in both walk-in refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Cheese and sauce were observed held at 50-51 degrees Fahrenheit in refrigerator #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, food had been in unit since the day prior. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Cheese, steak and corned beef were observed held at 51-56 degrees Fahrenheit on the right side of cold drawer #1 Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, food had been in unit since the day prior. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Deli meat and cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

(Sanitizer) was stored with plates and utensils. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Working containers of breadcrumbs are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of rice. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the dry storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Inspector observed the following:1. The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized,or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced.2. Prep unit #3 door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair.3. Cold drawer #1 was in poor repair, at time of inspection the right side of unit had an ambient temperature of 51F. PIC took unit out of service. TCS foods must not be stored in the unit until it has been serviced and is maintaining a temperature of 41°F or below.4. Refrigerator #1 was in poor repair, at time of inspection unit had an ambient temperature of 48F. PIC took unit out of service. TCS foods must not be stored in unit until it has been serviced and is maintaining a temperature of 41F or below. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris.1. Tables throughout kitchen2. Shelving throughout kitchen 3. Toaster4. Ovens 5. Fryers6. Hoods / ansul system 7. Broiler8. Grills New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
The condensate from the freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the equipment storage/ware washing area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by small flying insects throughout establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2025
